description: Ship combat: hull, shields, modules, weapons, damage

entries.content: A ship's hull integrity is reported in journal events as a float between 0.0 (destroyed) and 1.0 (full health). The HullDamage event fires on each damage tick. At 0.0 the ship is destroyed and the commander incurs the rebuy cost.
entries._justification: Phase 1 seed entry. Scale confirmed by journal observation -- Health field is normalized 0.0-1.0, not percent.

entries.content: The in-game HUD displays a hull warning when hull integrity drops below 25% (0.25 normalized). At this level the ship's status panel shows amber and voice alert fires. This is the threshold for HULL_CRITICAL_25 broadcast.
entries._justification: Week 7. Used by Week 9 Hull Triggers as the HULL_CRITICAL_25 threshold. Value 0.25 (normalized) confirmed by cockpit HUD amber transition.

entries.content: The in-game HUD displays a critical hull warning when hull integrity drops below 10% (0.10 normalized). At this level canopy cracks appear and cockpit damage effects increase. This is the threshold for HULL_CRITICAL_10 broadcast.
entries._justification: Week 7. Used by Week 9 Hull Triggers as the HULL_CRITICAL_10 threshold. Value 0.10 (normalized) confirmed by cockpit damage visual onset.

entries.content: Shields are reported via the Flags field in Status.json -- bit 3 (value 8) is set when shields are up and clear when shields are down. The journal also fires ShieldsDown and ShieldsUp events. Shield strength percentage is not directly in Status.json in all game versions; the Flags bit is the reliable cross-version indicator.
entries.source: Elite Dangerous journal documentation, in-game observation
entries._justification: Week 7. Needed by Week 9 SHIELDS_DOWN handler. Documents the Flags bit 3 approach used in Phase 1 status_reader.py.

entries.content: When a ship is destroyed, the commander can rebuy it for 5% of the ship's total insured value (hull + modules). The rebuy cost appears in the Died journal event payload as the field 'PilotName' is absent and rebuy fields may vary by game version. The standard insurance rate is 5%. Premium insurance (if purchased) reduces this.
entries.source: Elite Dangerous wiki, in-game Died event observation
entries._justification: Week 7. Foundation for Week 10 Rebuy Calculator. Standard insurance rate 5% confirmed from in-game store and community documentation.

entries.content: The standard rebuy cost in Elite Dangerous is 5% of the total insured value of the ship (hull + all fitted modules). The Rebuy Calculator uses this as the default rate. A commander can purchase premium insurance for a lower rate, but that data is not available in the journal -- Phase 2 uses 5% as the default.
entries.source: Elite Dangerous in-game insurance screen, community verification
entries._justification: Week 7. Rebuy Calculator (Feature 11, Week 10) reads this value. 5% is the universally documented standard rate.

entries.content: A fuel level below 25% of tank capacity is considered low. At this level the in-game HUD begins showing a fuel warning. OmniCOVAS broadcasts FUEL_LOW when fuel_main crosses below 25% of fuel_capacity in a downward direction. Expressed as a fraction: 0.25.
entries.source: in-game HUD observation, Phase 1 status_reader.py FUEL_LOW_THRESHOLD
entries._justification: Week 7. Matches the Phase 1 StatusReader FUEL_LOW_THRESHOLD = 0.25 constant so both the legacy sub-event and Phase 2 FUEL_LOW broadcast fire at the same level.

entries.content: A fuel level below 10% of tank capacity is considered critical. At this level the ship may not have sufficient fuel for another jump depending on jump range and fuel per jump. OmniCOVAS broadcasts FUEL_CRITICAL when fuel_main crosses below 10% of fuel_capacity in a downward direction. Expressed as a fraction: 0.10.
entries.source: in-game observation, community consensus on fuel emergency protocols
entries._justification: Week 7. 10% chosen as the critical threshold because below this level a ship is likely unable to complete even a minimum-range jump in some builds. Conservative and actionable.

entries.content: A ship's heat level is reported in Status.json as a float from 0.0 to 1.0 where 1.0 represents 100% heat (the overheat damage threshold). Values above 1.0 are possible during fuel scooping. OmniCOVAS issues a HEAT_WARNING when heat crosses above 0.75 (75%). The in-game HUD shows amber warnings starting around this level.
entries._justification: Week 7. Matches Phase 1 StatusReader HEAT_WARNING_THRESHOLD = 0.75. Medium confidence because the exact HUD threshold may vary slightly by ship/build. Flagged for in-game verification with thermal venting test.

entries.content: Module and hull damage from heat begins at 100% heat (1.0 normalized in Status.json). Above this level modules take incremental heat damage. The canopy may also crack at extreme heat levels. Fuel scooping commonly pushes heat to 110-130% temporarily.
entries.source: Elite Dangerous wiki, in-game fuel scoop observation
entries._justification: Week 7. Reference point for Heat Management (Feature 10, Week 9). Establishes that 1.0 is the damage onset, not just a display threshold.

entries.content: A ship module's health is reported in the Loadout journal event as a float between 0.0 (destroyed) and 1.0 (intact). OmniCOVAS broadcasts MODULE_DAMAGED when any module's health is below 0.8 (80%). At this level the module's performance begins to degrade noticeably. Expressed as a fraction: 0.8.
entries.source: Elite Dangerous module damage mechanics, in-game observation of module performance degradation onset
entries._justification: Week 8. Used by Feature 2 Module Health as the MODULE_DAMAGED threshold. 0.8 chosen as the standard community-documented degradation onset. Medium confidence: exact degradation percentage varies by module type.

entries.content: A ship module with health below 0.2 (20%) is critically damaged and at risk of failure. OmniCOVAS broadcasts MODULE_CRITICAL when any module drops below this level. At or near 0.0 the module is destroyed and non-functional. Expressed as a fraction: 0.2.
entries.source: Elite Dangerous module damage mechanics, in-game observation of module failure threshold
entries._justification: Week 8. Used by Feature 2 Module Health as the MODULE_CRITICAL threshold. 0.2 chosen as the point where module failure risk is high and immediate repair is advised. Medium confidence: failure probability at this health level is build-dependent.

entries.content: The Cargo journal event includes a Vessel field that indicates which context owns the cargo: Ship, SRV, or Suit. OmniCOVAS Pillar 1 tracks Ship cargo only. Events where Vessel is SRV or Suit must be dropped. Events where Vessel is absent (older game versions) are treated as Ship context.
entries.source: Elite Dangerous journal documentation, in-game observation of Cargo events across ship/SRV/Suit contexts
entries._justification: Week 8. Cargo handler must filter on Vessel == Ship to avoid polluting ship cargo_inventory with SRV or Suit cargo counts. Observed Vessel values: Ship, SRV, Suit.

entries.content: The Cargo journal event is the canonical source for a ship's cargo inventory. It fires at session start and whenever cargo changes (buy, sell, collect, eject). The Inventory array contains entries with Name (commodity internal name, lowercase) and Count (integer unit count). The top-level Count field is the total units across all commodities.
entries.source: Elite Dangerous journal documentation, in-game cargo transaction observation
entries._justification: Week 8. Documents the Cargo event structure used by cargo.py. Name field uses internal lowercase commodity names (e.g. gold, not Gold) matching the journal output directly.
